problem:
A survey of $120$ teachers determined the following:

$70$ had high blood pressure

$40$ had heart trouble

$20$ had both high blood pressure and heart trouble

What percent of the teachers surveyed had neither high blood pressure nor heart trouble?

classified_questions.question: What was the purpose of the survey and what was the sample size?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many teachers had high blood pressure?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many teachers had heart trouble?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many teachers had both high blood pressure and heart trouble?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many teachers had neither high blood pressure nor heart trouble?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you create a Venn diagram to visually represent the information given?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: If there were 200 teachers surveyed instead of 120, would the percentage of teachers without high blood pressure or heart trouble change?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: If 30 out of the 40 teachers with heart trouble were also overweight, what percentage of the total sample size is overweight and has heart trouble?
classified_questions.label: e
